rationale: The evidence shows a structured create-then-delete lifecycle for multiple scheduled tasks (A0/EV-001 create OnLogon/OnStartup tasks running calc.exe as SYSTEM; EV-002/EV-003 create additional tasks spawning cmd.exe; EV-004 registers a task via PowerShell for BUILTIN\Administrators at highest run level; EV-005 through EV-009 delete all created tasks within ~3.5 seconds). This pattern — rapid creation and cleanup of persistence-capable scheduled tasks from a Temp directory, driven by an encoded PowerShell parent command (EV-004 parent), is strongly consistent with automated security tool testing or red-team/atomic-test execution (e.g. Atomic Red Team T1053.005) rather than a live attacker establishing durable persistence. However, the use of calc.exe as a payload placeholder, credential passing in plaintext (EV-003/EV-008), execution from Temp, and encoded parent commands are all high-fidelity indicators that warrant confirmation of whether this was an authorized test. No evidence of task execution outcomes or lateral movement is present, and the cleanup reduces residual risk, but the activity cannot be closed as benign without authorization verification.
